spec_match.rationale: Lays out all four publisher×advertiser 3PC combinations with both pre-130 and post-130 behaviour. The leak narrative (one-sided cookie + debug key = cross-side identifier join) is technically accurate.

interactive.rationale: Era toggle, clickable matrix cells, diagram repaints per cell with stepped narrative. Real interaction, not just a static info panel.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises 2x2 matrix with per-cell explanation of the cross-side identifier leak and the strip rule — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: All four cells covered including the never-leaked case (both 3PC blocked) and the safe case (both 3PC allowed). Each cell has its own narrative.

references.rationale: chromestatus link plus spec PR link — good.

design_system.rationale: Uses hardcoded hex colors #ffeae9 and #eaf6ef for cell background tinting (lines 310-311). CLAUDE.md rule #5 forbids raw hex — must use CSS variables (e.g. color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-rose) 10%, transparent) or a tinted background variable).

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex backgrounds with CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Lines 310-311 of the JS set cell.style.background to '#ffeae9' (rose tint) and '#eaf6ef' (emerald tint). CLAUDE.md rule #5 prohibits raw hex; switch to color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-rose) 12%, var(--bg-paper)) and the emerald equivalent, or add named tint variables to the inline style block.

openQuestions.title: Show the actual report JSON per cell
openQuestions.detail: The matrix shows verdicts (OK / LEAK / STRIPPED) but not the resulting JSON the browser would emit per cell. Adding a small report-shape readout in the diagram panel would close the loop between 'what happened' and 'what arrives at the reporting origin'.

openQuestions.title: Annotate the symmetric one-sided cell
openQuestions.detail: pub-block + ad-allow says 'symmetric to the other one-sided case' in the leak narrative — but the asymmetry is interesting (trigger_debug_key joins shop-side ID to news.example source visit, the reverse direction). Spell out the symmetry rather than gesturing at it.
